world_assessment: A quiet news week with no macro regime changes. Key datapoints: (1) Samsung nominating Austin for a $17B chip plant directly confirms our Semiconductor Supercycle thesis — the industry is investing massively in supply capacity, validating the multi-year shortage narrative. (2) Elliott Management exploring a $1B+ SPAC confirms capital markets activity remains elevated, supporting our GS thesis. (3) Porsche CEO guiding 50%+ EV sales by 2030 reinforces the EV transition structural trend. (4) Ford's DOJ criminal probe closure removes a meaningful overhang — clean story now. (5) Analyst upgrades on F (Benchmark to $14, Deutsche Bank to $12) provide external validation. (6) Jon Najarian selling F calls is a mild cautionary signal but he's a short-term trader, not a thesis investor. Overall: no thesis-breaking news. Portfolio positioning remains well-aligned with macro themes. Cash at 51% is high but with no clear new catalysts and 12 trades already executed, discipline favors holding rather than forcing deployment into thin news.

thesis_updates.notes: Samsung's $17B Austin plant nomination is direct confirmation of the semiconductor supercycle thesis. The industry is so supply-constrained that competitors are spending $17B on new capacity — this doesn't happen unless demand is structural and sustained. TSM remains the irreplaceable leading-edge foundry. RSI=64, ADX=43, still above SMA50 — technically sound despite MACD turning bearish. The bearish MACD is a consolidation signal, not a reversal. Holding.

Clean energy thesis remains policy-supported under Biden but technicals are softening: MACD bearish, OBV falling, ADX=25 (weakening trend conviction). Down 4.6% from entry. No fundamental news this week to reassess. The thesis invalidation condition (clean energy policy reversal) has not occurred. Monitoring closely — if OBV continues falling and price breaks below SMA50, will reassess.

Blue Wave reflation thesis intact. No new stimulus legislation news but the political environment hasn't changed. RSI=66, MACD bullish, above SMA50 — technically constructive despite OBV falling. The OBV decline is worth watching but insufficient to exit. Holding.

Short thesis is working: +4.8% gain. RSI=36, below SMA50, MACD bearish, OBV falling — all confirming the structural decline thesis. RSI=36 is approaching the Lesson 6 trigger zone (RSI<30 = take partial profits). Not at that threshold yet. Will take partial profits if RSI reaches 30 or price approaches lower Bollinger Band. Monitoring.

The speculative Microsoft acquisition article (AMD/NVDA/MU) is noise — not actionable thesis input. The real news is Samsung's $17B plant, which confirms the memory supercycle. MU's thesis is standalone: DRAM/NAND price cycle turning, hyperscaler customers locking in capacity. MACD bearish and OBV falling are mild concerns. RSI=62, ADX=39, above SMA50 — trend still intact. Holding.

Infrastructure thesis intact. No new legislative catalyst but the Biden infrastructure agenda remains on track. RSI=64, MACD bullish, above SMA50 — technically sound despite OBV falling. The OBV decline across multiple positions this week may reflect broader market rotation rather than stock-specific weakness. Holding.

Agriculture reflation + infrastructure thesis intact. RSI=68, near upper Bollinger Band — approaching overbought. MACD bullish and ADX=34 suggest trend strength but the upper BB proximity warrants caution on adding. Not a reason to exit. Holding.

Elliott Management's $1B+ SPAC is direct confirmation of the capital markets boom thesis. SPAC activity at record levels, M&A pipeline building — exactly the environment where GS earns outsized fees. RSI=67, ADX=36, above SMA50. MACD bearish is the only concern but ADX strength suggests institutional trend is intact. Holding.

Jon Najarian's public endorsement is signal confirmation. RSI=64, MACD bullish, ADX=44, OBV rising — the only position with OBV rising and MACD bullish simultaneously. Technically the cleanest setup in the book. Government and commercial Foundry thesis intact. Holding.

DOJ criminal emissions probe closure removes a meaningful legal overhang — this is materially positive. Benchmark raises PT to $14 (21% upside from current), Deutsche Bank raises to $12. Analyst community repricing higher. RSI=70 is approaching overbought, and Najarian selling calls is a short-term trader's hedge, not a thesis signal. OBV rising and MACD bullish confirm institutional buying. Thesis is strengthening, not weakening. Holding but watching RSI — above 75 would trigger a partial profit consideration.

theme_updates.name: Semiconductor Supercycle
theme_updates.reason: Already at maximum score (5/5). Samsung's $17B Austin plant is the strongest possible confirmation — the industry is in a structural multi-year supercycle. Cannot increment further but conviction is at peak.

lesson_updates.reason: VZ short approaching trigger zone (RSI=36) but not yet at RSI<30. Lesson is about to be tested in real-time. No increment or decrement yet — will update when the trigger fires or fails.

belief_updates.name: Technical Confirmation Before Shorting
belief_updates.description: Never short a stock that is still trading above its SMA50 with rising RSI and bullish MACD, regardless of how compelling the bear thesis appears. The market can remain irrational longer than a position can remain solvent. Wait for confirmed technical breakdown — below SMA50, declining RSI (ideally below 50), and bearish MACD — before entering short positions. This applies even to structurally declining businesses. Momentum kills premature shorts.

weekly_summary: Week of Feb 8, 2021: Quiet macro week with no regime-changing news. Portfolio performing well at +5.3% vs S&P +4.1% (+130bps alpha). All 10 active theses remain valid. Key confirmation: Samsung's $17B Austin chip plant validates Semiconductor Supercycle thesis. Elliott SPAC validates GS capital markets thesis. Ford DOJ probe closure removes legal overhang — thesis strengthening. VZ short working with RSI=36 approaching partial-profit trigger zone. No new positions opened — trade discipline applied with 12 trades already executed. Cash held at 51% pending higher-conviction catalysts. First investment belief formalized from Lesson 3 (Technical Confirmation Before Shorting). Portfolio well-aligned with macro themes entering mid-February.
